2026-04-14.name: Houston
2026-04-14.text: On April 14, 1970, Mission Control in Houston received the famous Apollo 13 distress call after an oxygen tank failure crippled the spacecraft on its way to the Moon. The line is often remembered as "Houston, we have a problem," but the original transmission was even more immediate: "Houston, we've had a problem."

2026-04-14.name: Monaco
2026-04-14.text: On April 14, 1929, the inaugural Monaco Grand Prix was held on the streets of the principality, beginning one of motor racing's most iconic events. The race helped turn Monaco into a permanent symbol of glamorous, high-risk circuit racing.

2026-04-14.name: Tbilisi
2026-04-14.text: On April 14, 1978, thousands of people gathered in Tbilisi to protest Soviet attempts to weaken the status of the Georgian language. The demonstrations succeeded, and the date is still remembered in Georgia as a symbol of linguistic identity and civic resistance.

2026-04-14.name: Dhaka
2026-04-14.text: On April 14, Dhaka becomes one of the main centers of Pohela Boishakh, the Bengali New Year, with processions, music, and public celebrations across the city. The festivities around Ramna Park and the University of Dhaka have made the capital one of the date's most recognizable cultural focal points.

2026-04-14.name: Chibok
2026-04-14.text: On April 14, 2014, Chibok became known around the world when Boko Haram abducted 276 schoolgirls from the town. The event drew sustained international attention and turned this relatively remote place into a global symbol of grief, outrage, and demands for accountability.

2026-04-15.name: Boston
2026-04-15.text: On April 15, 2013, the Boston Marathon bombing turned Boston into the center of a major domestic terror attack and the days-long manhunt that followed. The attack permanently linked the city to one of the most consequential acts of violence in recent American public life.

2026-04-15.name: Bari
2026-04-15.text: On April 15, 1071, Bari, the last Byzantine possession in southern Italy, surrendered to Robert Guiscard after a long siege. The city marks the end of Byzantine rule in the region.

2026-04-15.name: Busan
2026-04-15.text: On April 15, 2002, Air China Flight 129 crashed on approach to Gimhae International Airport in Busan. The disaster made the city the focus of one of South Korea's deadliest aviation accidents.

2026-04-15.name: Marrakesh
2026-04-15.text: On April 15, 1994, the Marrakesh Agreement establishing the World Trade Organization was adopted in Marrakesh. The city gave its name to one of the key agreements shaping the modern global trading system.

2026-04-15.name: Paris
2026-04-15.text: On April 15, 2019, a major fire severely damaged Notre-Dame de Paris. Paris became the focus of worldwide attention as people watched one of its best-known landmarks burn.

2026-04-16.name: Skopje
2026-04-16.text: On April 16, 1346, Stefan Dusan was crowned emperor in Skopje. The city served as the ceremonial center of a major medieval Balkan empire at a decisive moment in regional history.

2026-04-16.name: Salta
2026-04-16.text: On April 16, 1582, Spanish conquistador Hernando de Lerma founded the settlement of Salta. That date marks the beginning of one of northwestern Argentina's most important colonial-era cities.

2026-04-16.name: Blacksburg
2026-04-16.text: On April 16, 2007, the Virginia Tech shooting made Blacksburg the site of the deadliest school shooting in U.S. history. The town became the center of a tragedy that reshaped national debates over campus safety and gun violence.

2026-04-16.name: Athens
2026-04-16.text: On April 16, 2003, the Treaty of Accession was signed in Athens to admit ten new states to the European Union. The city briefly became the stage for one of the EU's largest single expansions.

2026-04-16.name: Valletta
2026-04-16.text: On April 16, 1942, King George VI awarded the George Cross to Malta in recognition of the island's collective wartime bravery. The decoration became one of the defining symbols of Malta's experience in World War II.

2026-04-17.name: Kaunas
2026-04-17.text: On April 17, 1362, Kaunas Castle fell to the Teutonic Order after a long siege. Kaunas entered the historical record as a frontier stronghold in the medieval Baltic conflicts.

2026-04-17.name: San Juan
2026-04-17.text: On April 17, 1797, British forces attacked San Juan in one of the largest invasions of Spanish territories in the Americas. The city's defenses helped repel the assault and preserve Spanish control.

2026-04-17.name: Damascus
2026-04-17.text: On April 17, 1946, the last French troops withdrew from Syria, and Damascus became the capital of a fully independent state. The date is still commemorated in Syria as Evacuation Day.

2026-04-17.name: Maputo
2026-04-17.text: On April 17, 1992, the tanker Katina P was deliberately run aground off Maputo, causing a major oil spill. The incident linked the city to one of the notable environmental accidents in the western Indian Ocean.

2026-04-17.name: Windsor
2026-04-17.text: On April 17, 2021, the funeral of Prince Philip took place at St George's Chapel in Windsor Castle. Windsor became the ceremonial focus of a globally watched royal event.

2026-04-18.name: Vatican City
2026-04-18.text: On April 18, 1506, the cornerstone of the current St. Peter's Basilica was laid in Vatican City. The date marks the beginning of one of the most famous church-building projects in the world.

2026-04-18.name: Addis Ababa
2026-04-18.text: On April 18, 1972, East African Airways Flight 720 crashed during a rejected takeoff from Addis Ababa Bole International Airport. Addis Ababa became the site of one of the most serious air disasters in East African aviation history.

2026-04-18.name: Tokyo
2026-04-18.text: On April 18, 1942, Tokyo was among the Japanese cities bombed in the Doolittle Raid. The attack made the capital a central symbol in a dramatic turning point of the Pacific War.

2026-04-18.name: The Hague
2026-04-18.text: On April 18, 1946, the International Court of Justice held its inaugural meeting in The Hague. The city further strengthened its role as one of the world's main centers for international law.

2026-04-18.name: Bandung
2026-04-18.text: On April 18, 1955, leaders from twenty-nine nations gathered in Bandung for the Asian-African Conference. The city became permanently associated with postcolonial solidarity and the rise of the Non-Aligned world.

2026-04-19.name: Caracas
2026-04-19.text: On April 19, 1810, people in Caracas removed the Spanish governor and installed a local junta. The city became one of the first major centers of the independence movement in Spanish South America.

2026-04-19.name: Warsaw
2026-04-19.text: On April 19, 1943, the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising began after German forces entered the ghetto. Warsaw became the site of one of the most powerful acts of Jewish resistance during World War II.

2026-04-19.name: Basel
2026-04-19.text: On April 19, 1943, Albert Hofmann deliberately took LSD in Basel and made the bicycle ride later celebrated as Bicycle Day. The city is tied to one of the strangest and most widely referenced episodes in modern chemical history.

2026-04-19.name: Berlin
2026-04-19.text: On April 19, 1999, the German Bundestag returned to Berlin. The move confirmed the city once again as the working capital of reunified Germany.

2026-04-19.name: Santiago
2026-04-19.text: On April 19, 1925, Colo-Colo was founded in Santiago and went on to become Chile's most successful football club. The city is therefore tied to one of the country's most important sporting institutions.

2026-04-20.name: Minneapolis
2026-04-20.text: On April 20, 2021, Minneapolis was the city where Derek Chauvin was found guilty of murdering George Floyd. The verdict was watched around the world as a rare moment of legal accountability after months of protest and grief. That made Minneapolis a defining place in one of the most consequential civil rights stories of the decade.

2026-04-20.name: Geneva
2026-04-20.text: On April 20, 1946, the League of Nations was formally dissolved in Geneva, closing the chapter on the first major attempt at a permanent international peace organization. Most of its remaining responsibilities passed to the newly established United Nations. That gives Geneva a direct link to the handoff between two eras of global diplomacy.

2026-04-20.name: Motegi
2026-04-20.text: On April 20, 2008, Motegi became the setting for a motorsport breakthrough when Danica Patrick won the Indy Japan 300. She became the first woman ever to win an IndyCar race, turning the town's circuit into the site of a landmark result. The moment still stands as one of the most celebrated firsts in modern auto racing.

2026-04-20.name: Timbuktu
2026-04-20.text: On April 20, 1828, Rene Caillie reached Timbuktu, becoming the first non-Muslim European known to return alive after visiting the city. For centuries the name had carried an almost mythical reputation in Europe as a distant and fabulously rich place. His arrival helped turn Timbuktu from legend into a documented destination in global geography.

2026-04-20.name: Leipzig
2026-04-20.text: On April 20, 1945, United States troops captured Leipzig as Nazi Germany was collapsing in the final weeks of World War II. The city briefly became one of the most important urban prizes of the Western Allied advance into central Germany before it was later ceded to the Soviet occupation zone. That handover gives Leipzig a revealing place in the geopolitics of victory at the war's end.

2026-04-21.name: Rome
2026-04-21.text: On April 21, according to Roman tradition, Romulus founded Rome. The date became so important that Romans later celebrated it as the city's birthday, tying imperial grandeur to a single mythic origin story. Few cities in the world have had a founding legend with such a long political and cultural afterlife.

2026-04-21.name: Brasilia
2026-04-21.text: On April 21, 1960, Brasilia was officially inaugurated as Brazil's new capital. The move shifted political power away from coastal Rio de Janeiro and planted a purpose-built modernist city deep in the country's interior. Its vast avenues and monumental government buildings were meant to symbolize a new national future.

2026-04-21.name: Seattle
2026-04-21.text: On April 21, 1962, Seattle opened the Century 21 Exposition, the city's World's Fair. The event helped recast Seattle as a forward-looking center of aerospace, design, and technology during the Space Age. Landmarks like the Space Needle still carry that burst of futuristic ambition.

2026-04-21.name: La Paz
2026-04-21.text: On April 21, 1993, Bolivia's Supreme Court in La Paz sentenced former dictator Luis Garcia Meza to 30 years in prison without parole. The ruling became one of Latin America's most notable examples of a former military strongman being held accountable at home. That gave La Paz a distinct place in the region's long struggle with dictatorship and justice.

2026-04-21.name: Bangkok
2026-04-21.text: On April 21, 1782, the city of Rattanakosin, now Bangkok, was founded on the eastern bank of the Chao Phraya River. That moment marked the start of the Chakri dynasty and the beginning of Bangkok's rise as Thailand's political and ceremonial heart. Much of the city's identity still traces back to this deliberate royal founding.

2026-04-22.name: Paris
2026-04-22.text: On April 22, 2016, leaders gathered in Paris to sign the Paris Agreement, one of the most ambitious global climate accords yet attempted. The city had already lent its name to the deal, but the signing fixed Paris even more firmly in the story of international climate diplomacy. It is now one of the places most closely associated with the politics of global warming.

2026-04-22.name: Haifa
2026-04-22.text: On April 22, 1948, Jewish forces captured Haifa during the civil war phase of the conflict in Mandatory Palestine. The battle turned the port city into one of the decisive urban prizes of that turbulent spring. Haifa's harbor and mixed population made the struggle there especially consequential.

2026-04-22.name: Guadalajara
2026-04-22.text: On April 22, 1992, a chain of gas explosions tore through Guadalajara, devastating streets and neighborhoods across the city. The disaster killed hundreds and became one of the worst urban infrastructure catastrophes in modern Mexican history. Guadalajara is still remembered in connection with how suddenly an everyday cityscape turned catastrophic.

2026-04-22.name: Kolkata
2026-04-22.text: On April 22, 1969, the formation of the Communist Party of India (Marxist-Leninist) was announced at a mass rally in Kolkata. The city, already famous for radical politics and intellectual debate, became the launch point for one of the most consequential far-left movements in modern India. That moment tied Kolkata even more closely to the turbulent political energy of the late 1960s.

2026-04-22.name: Jayapura
2026-04-22.text: On April 22, 1944, Allied forces landed in the Hollandia area of New Guinea, in the region now centered on Jayapura. The operation helped break Japanese positions in the southwest Pacific and pulled this remote coastal city into the wider geography of World War II. Jayapura's modern identity still sits atop that wartime strategic history.

2026-04-23.name: San Diego
2026-04-23.text: On April 23, 2005, Jawed Karim uploaded Me at the zoo, the first video ever posted to YouTube, after filming it at the San Diego Zoo. That brief clip helped mark the beginning of a platform that would transform entertainment, politics, and everyday life online. It gives San Diego a small but oddly foundational place in internet history.

2026-04-23.name: Windhoek
2026-04-23.text: On April 23, 1990, Namibia entered the United Nations and the Commonwealth only weeks after independence, with Windhoek at the center of the new state's political life. The moment confirmed the country's place on the world stage after a long struggle against South African rule. As capital, Windhoek became the symbolic front door of that transition.

2026-04-23.name: Algiers
2026-04-23.text: On April 23, 1961, French generals launched the Algiers putsch in an attempt to stop Algerian independence. For a brief moment, Algiers became the center of one of the last great crises of France's colonial era. The failed coup exposed how violently the empire was unraveling.

2026-04-23.name: Prague
2026-04-23.text: On April 23, 1951, American journalist William N. Oatis was arrested in Prague by the communist authorities of Czechoslovakia. The case became an early Cold War flashpoint over press freedom, espionage accusations, and show-trial politics. Prague was thus pulled into the wider contest between East and West not just as a capital, but as a symbolic courtroom.

2026-04-23.name: Manila
2026-04-23.text: On April 23, 1946, Manuel Roxas was elected the last president of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, in the final stretch before full independence. That made Manila the political stage for a pivotal handoff between colonial rule and the postwar republic. The city was where the future direction of the Philippines began to take clearer shape.

2026-04-24.name: Dahab
2026-04-24.text: On April 24, 2006, bombings struck the Red Sea resort town of Dahab during a busy holiday period. The attack shattered the easygoing image of one of Sinai's best-known beach destinations and drew worldwide attention to the town. Dahab's name has since carried both its backpacker mystique and the memory of that violence.

2026-04-24.name: Dublin
2026-04-24.text: On April 24, 1916, rebels launched the Easter Rising in Dublin and proclaimed an Irish Republic. The insurrection was militarily crushed, but it transformed the political future of Ireland and turned the city center into historic ground. Few urban uprisings have reshaped national memory as completely as this one.

2026-04-24.name: Santorini
2026-04-24.text: On April 24, 1944, British commandos raided Santorini to destroy an Axis radio station during World War II. The operation briefly placed the famous Aegean island inside the covert geography of the Mediterranean war. Santorini is better known for whitewashed cliffs and tourism, which makes that wartime episode all the more surprising.

2026-04-24.name: Banjul
2026-04-24.text: On April 24, 1970, The Gambia became a republic within the Commonwealth, with Dawda Jawara as its first president. As the capital, Banjul stood at the formal center of that constitutional turning point. It marked the moment the small West African state moved from monarchy under the British crown to a republic of its own.

2026-04-24.name: Santo Domingo
2026-04-24.text: On April 24, 1965, civil war broke out in Santo Domingo after supporters of deposed president Juan Bosch rose against the ruling triumvirate. The capital quickly became the heart of a conflict that drew in foreign intervention and Cold War anxieties. Santo Domingo's streets turned into the main battleground for the Dominican Republic's fight over its political future.

2026-04-25.name: Lisbon
2026-04-25.text: On April 25, 1974, Lisbon awoke to the Carnation Revolution, the military-led uprising that brought down Portugal's Estado Novo dictatorship. Crowds filled the city and placed carnations in soldiers' rifles, creating one of the century's most enduring images of a relatively peaceful revolution. Lisbon became the unmistakable face of Portugal's democratic rebirth.

2026-04-25.name: Obelisk of Axum
2026-04-25.text: On April 25, 2005, the final piece of the Obelisk of Axum was returned to Ethiopia after decades in Italy. The monument had long stood as one of the most recognizable symbols of the ancient Aksumite world, so its return carried weight far beyond archaeology. It turned Axum into a modern story of restitution, memory, and national pride.

2026-04-25.name: Managua
2026-04-25.text: On April 25, 1990, Violeta Chamorro took office in Managua as president of Nicaragua. She became the first woman elected in her own right to lead a country in the Americas, giving the capital a place in a larger political first. Managua thus became the setting for a major democratic handover after years of conflict and polarization.

2026-04-25.name: Berkeley Heights
2026-04-25.text: On April 25, 1954, Bell Telephone Laboratories publicly demonstrated the first practical solar cell at its New Jersey research facilities in what is now Berkeley Heights. That breakthrough helped turn solar power from laboratory curiosity into a credible technological future. A quiet suburban township thus became part of the early history of renewable energy.

2026-04-25.name: Kathmandu
2026-04-25.text: On April 25, 2015, Kathmandu became the global focal point of the devastating Nepal earthquake. Images of collapsed temples, damaged squares, and emergency rescues in the capital helped define how the disaster was understood around the world. The city remains central to the memory of one of the deadliest Himalayan earthquakes in modern times.
